verses.text: Solomon begins to build on Mount Moriah, at Ornan's threshingfloor, where the LORD appeared to David — and where Abraham once offered Isaac. The temple rises on the ground of atonement. True worship is founded where judgment met mercy through sacrifice.

Solomon begins to build on Mount Moriah, at Ornan's threshingfloor, where the LORD appeared to David — and where Abraham once offered Isaac. The temple rises on the ground of atonement. True worship is founded where judgment met mercy through sacrifice.

The house is measured and lined with cedar overlaid with fine gold, adorned with palm trees and chains. Beauty and cost mark the work. What is built for God is made glorious, the best that skill and wealth can offer.

The house is measured and lined with cedar overlaid with fine gold, adorned with palm trees and chains. Beauty and cost mark the work. What is built for God is made glorious, the best that skill and wealth can offer.

The house is measured and lined with cedar overlaid with fine gold, adorned with palm trees and chains. Beauty and cost mark the work. What is built for God is made glorious, the best that skill and wealth can offer.

The greater house is garnished with precious stones and the purest gold, even the nails of gold. The closer to God's presence, the more glorious the work. Nothing is too precious for the place where God meets His people.

The greater house is garnished with precious stones and the purest gold, even the nails of gold. The closer to God's presence, the more glorious the work. Nothing is too precious for the place where God meets His people.

The greater house is garnished with precious stones and the purest gold, even the nails of gold. The closer to God's presence, the more glorious the work. Nothing is too precious for the place where God meets His people.

The greater house is garnished with precious stones and the purest gold, even the nails of gold. The closer to God's presence, the more glorious the work. Nothing is too precious for the place where God meets His people.

In the most holy place stand two great cherubim of gold, their wings outspread across the room. The angelic guardians attend the throne of God. His presence is holy and awesome, surrounded by the worship of heaven even in this earthly picture.

In the most holy place stand two great cherubim of gold, their wings outspread across the room. The angelic guardians attend the throne of God. His presence is holy and awesome, surrounded by the worship of heaven even in this earthly picture.

The veil is hung, embroidered with cherubim, and two great pillars are set up — Jachin, 'He shall establish,' and Boaz, 'In Him is strength.' Their names preach to every worshiper. Our standing and our strength are not our own but rest wholly in God.
